---
title: "Tripple Boot (Ubuntu 10.10, Win7, Win7) - Grub finds Win 7 but boots wrong"
date: 2011-01-27
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by BurtaN on 2011-01-27
Hey,

I've just installed Ubuntu 10.10 on my Laptop, which I use for music-production and universal stuff. That's why I have two instances of Win7. When installing Grub 2, it correctly detects all OSs but no matter which Win7 entry I select, it always boots /dev/sda2. 

```
#
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE
#
# It is automatically generated by grub-mkconfig using templates
# from /etc/grub.d and settings from /etc/default/grub
#

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/00_header ###
if [ -s $prefix/grubenv ]; then
  set have_grubenv=true
  load_env
fi
set default="0"
if [ "${prev_saved_entry}" ]; then
  set saved_entry="${prev_saved_entry}"
  save_env saved_entry
  set prev_saved_entry=
  save_env prev_saved_entry
  set boot_once=true
fi

function savedefault {
  if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then
    saved_entry="${chosen}"
    save_env saved_entry
  fi
}

function recordfail {
  set recordfail=1
  if [ -n "${have_grubenv}" ]; then if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then save_env recordfail; fi; fi
}

function load_video {
  insmod vbe
  insmod vga
}

insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
if loadfont /usr/share/grub/unicode.pf2 ; then
  set gfxmode=640x480
  load_video
  insmod gfxterm
fi
terminal_output gfxterm
insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
set locale_dir=($root)/boot/grub/locale
set lang=de
insmod gettext
if [ "${recordfail}" = 1 ]; then
  set timeout=-1
else
  set timeout=10
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/00_header ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###
set menu_color_normal=white/black
set menu_color_highlight=black/light-gray
### END /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-24-generic' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-24-generic root=UUID=2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af ro   quiet splash
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-24-generic
}
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-24-generic (recovery mode)' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
	echo	'Loading Linux 2.6.35-24-generic ...'
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-24-generic root=UUID=2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af ro single 
	echo	'Loading initial ramdisk ...'
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-24-generic
}
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-22-generic' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-22-generic root=UUID=2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af ro   quiet splash
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-22-generic (recovery mode)' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
	echo	'Loading Linux 2.6.35-22-generic ...'
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-22-generic root=UUID=2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af ro single 
	echo	'Loading initial ramdisk ...'
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}
### END /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###
### END /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin
}
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+, serial console 115200)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos4)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 2bc034e9-2f6f-489d-be6c-2dcb3ae917af
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin console=ttyS0,115200n8
}
### END /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###
menuentry "Windows 7 (loader) (on /dev/sda1)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ntfs
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set e07888237887f716
	chainloader +1
}
menuentry "Windows 7 (loader) (on /dev/sda2)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ntfs
	set root='(hd0,msdos2)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set e07888237887f716
	chainloader +1
}
### END /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###
# This file provides an easy way to add custom menu entries.  Simply type the
# menu entries you want to add after this comment.  Be careful not to change
# the 'exec tail' line above.
### END /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###
if [ -f  $prefix/custom.cfg ]; then
  source $prefix/custom.cfg;
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###
```

As you can see, the menu entry /dev/sda1 should boot "set root='(hd0,msdos1)'" but still sda2 is bootet. When installing BootUS, I can correctly boot both Win7 partitions, thus it must be Grub 2, which causes the problem.

Thanks in advance!
BurtaN

---

### Post by friv_livs on 2011-01-27
Change chainloader +1 in the section below to chainloader +2

menuentry "Windows 7 (loader) (on /dev/sda2)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ntfs
	set root='(hd0,msdos2)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set e07888237887f716
	chainloader +1

This is only temporary and someone else will have to help you with setting the files grub2 reads to generate your grub.cfg.

Also: it appears both windows partitions have the same uuid.

---

### Post by BurtaN on 2011-01-27
Hey,

I've kept on reading about Grub 2 and tested your advice. Changing chainloader to +2 didn't work as the non-booting partition is sda1. What works is to delete

```
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set e07888237887f716
``` That seems to be the clue. I'll to add the entry manually by using 40_custom.

Thanks for the UUID hint :)

---
